mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate falling below 7.0 percent signals labor market tightening and rising consumer income, which increases corporate earnings expectations and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in greater than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE crosses below the 7.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which directly increases corporate revenue expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns positive.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index signals weakening manufacturing output and reduced capacity utilization, which triggers short-term profit warnings and negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth coincides with positive 5-day returns in greater than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation exceeding 235.0 triggers expectations of Federal Reserve quantitative easing tapering, which increases market discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above threshold yields positive 20-day returns in greater than 65 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms sustainable job growth, which raises consumer income and drives positive quarterly equity returns through higher corporate sales.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in greater than 55 percent of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate profit margins and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 against 20-day forward returns remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 1.0 percent month-over-month signals robust factory utilization, which reduces supply chain bottlenecks and triggers positive short-term equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO MoM growth above 1.0 percent is followed by negative 5-day returns in greater than 60 percent of instances.
